evidences.title: Meta Platforms Q3 2025: AI Expansion Drives Strong ...
evidences.content: Meta Platforms (NASDAQ: META) delivered a powerful third quarter, with revenue climbing 26% year-over-year to $51.2 billion. Advertising contributed $50.1 billion, supported by robust engagement across Facebook, Instagram, and Threads. Despite a 32% rise in expenses, operating margins held firm at 40%, highlighting Meta’s balance between rapid AI investment and profitability. [...] Free cash flow reached $10.6 billion, with $3.2 billion in stock buybacks and $1.3 billion in dividends. Li noted Meta will “invest aggressively” in 2026 to meet surging compute demand, meaning expenses will “grow at a significantly faster percentage rate” next year. On the operational front, AI continues to lift engagement and monetization efficiency. The Lattice architecture has consolidated 100 ad models, boosting conversions by nearly 3%. Business messaging revenue from click-to-WhatsApp ads jumped 60% year-over-year, and generative tools like Vibes and AI-generated music are expanding creative options for advertisers. evidences.title: Meta Q3 2025: 26% ad revenue growth, AI investments pay off | Eric Seufert posted on the topic | LinkedIn
evidences.content: Meta shares plunged about 12% on Oct. 29. The Company posted quarterly revenue of $51.2 billion, a 26% YoY rise, and net income of $2.7 billion, which is below analysts’ expectations. The decline in the share price is not explained by the net income. Indeed, the net income shortfall is attributed to an accounting adjustment to reflect future tax liability. In Meta’s third-quarter 2025 earnings call, CFO Susan Li emphasized the company’s intention to invest aggressively in AI. « Our current expectation is that capex dollar growth will be notably larger in 2026 than 2025. We also anticipate total expenses will grow at a significantly faster percentage rate in 2026 than 2025, with growth driven primarily by infrastructure costs, including incremental cloud expenses and depreciation. » [...] Recent moves by Meta Platforms clearly show that the company is going all in on the race for dominance in artificial intelligence.
